learnings.text: Fillout API stores emoji-labeled sentiment questions as full text strings (e.g., '5 = 🚀 Excited / All-in' not just '5'). D1.2 analysis script must parse these to extract numeric scores for sentiment analysis. Pattern: 'N = emoji Text' - extract N.

This form uses a screening question (dGZw) to filter attendees vs. non-attendees. D1.2 should filter out 'No' responses from all analysis. Consider standardizing this pattern for future event surveys.
Screening questions in Fillout forms don't always filter API responses - ineligible responses may be included in the dataset, requiring manual filtering
Survey with 100% uniform sentiment responses indicates selection bias, tribal signaling, or flawed design - sentiment data is effectively broken and cannot support correlation analysis
'Scratching the surface' is not a beginner state but a permanent condition for anyone staying current with AI - curriculum should reflect this

Bug found in fillout_client.py: _detect_account() was returning account name string but was assigned to api_key variable, causing 400 errors. Fixed by returning API key directly instead of account name.
Dashboard should include data quality warnings section when anomalies detected (e.g., 100% max sentiment, small sample size) (D2.1)
Sentiment scale with 100% uniform max values requires data quality warnings in dashboard and analysis - likely tribal signaling not actual sentiment (D2.1)
Analysis synthesis needs explicit Level Upper insight markers (🔮) to distinguish novel perspectives from standard findings (D2.1)
